div1.entryFree:
Foil, subst. 1) defeat, miscarriage: one sudden f. shall never breed mistrust, H6A III, 3, 11. take my soul, before that England give the French the f. V, 3, 23.
2) blemish, shortcoming: some defect in her did quarrel with the noblest grace she owed and put it to the f. Tp. III, 1, 46. yet must Antony no way excuse his --s, when we do bear so great weight in his lightness, Ant. I, 4, 24 (M. Edd. soils).
